title: Rotator cuff symptom review

red_flags.text: Requires direct clinician-patient decisions and lists infection, unreduced dislocation, tumour, traumatic acute cuff tear, and inflammatory disease as urgent concern contexts.
red_flags.rationale: Supports entered shoulder red flags for rotator-cuff symptoms; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires direct clinician-patient decisions and lists infection, unreduced dislocation, tumour, traumatic acute cuff tear, and inflammatory disease as urgent concern contexts.

source.title: Shoulder Pain: Diagnosis, Treatment and Referral Guidelines for Primary, Community and Intermediate Care

exact_section.heading: Shared Decision Making and Urgent Red Flag Referrals for the Shoulder

red_flags.text: Supports rotator-cuff or impingement terminology and history covering hand dominance, occupation, activity, pain location, radiation, onset, duration, aggravating factors, trauma, comorbidities, red flags, and passive external rotation.
red_flags.rationale: Supports clinician assessment of subacromial shoulder pain for rotator-cuff symptoms; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports rotator-cuff or impingement terminology and history covering hand dominance, occupation, activity, pain location, radiation, onset, duration, aggravating factors, trauma, comorbidities, red flags, and passive external rotation.

source.title: Shoulder Pain: Diagnosis, Treatment and Referral Guidelines for Primary, Community and Intermediate Care

exact_section.heading: Subacromial Shoulder Pain — definition and diagnosis

investigations.text: Supports selected two-view radiography, states ultrasound or MRI is rarely indicated without an agreed specialist pathway, and links persistent symptoms to clinician-arranged referral.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-led imaging and referral context for rotator-cuff symptoms; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports selected two-view radiography, states ultrasound or MRI is rarely indicated without an agreed specialist pathway, and links persistent symptoms to clinician-arranged referral.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Onset, trauma, pain arc, night pain, weakness, active and passive motion, examination, imaging and functional impact are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for rotator-cuff symptoms.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
